TextENGNew: Evening fell, and we were just sitting down to dinner when the phone rang.
My mother, who had only just finished setting the table, hurried toward the front door.
``Huh?  Who could that be.  Don't tell me it's something to do with Rena?!``
Sure enough — it was a call from Rena's father.
``H-Hello?  Good evening!``
``Sorry to call so late.  I was wondering if Reina might be with you.  She ran out of the house in a hurry earlier this evening and hasn't come back.``
``She ran out of the house?!  What was she like when she left?``
``Headlong — the way she gets when she's up to her pranks.  I assumed she was headed to a friend's house, but...``
``...That settles it.  Someone picked up the white magatama!!``
``I'm so sorry I'm late!!``
``...What's the situation?``
``We just got permission to go into Rena's room.  Judging by how she was at school, she must have left some kind of clue behind.``
``Right.  Come to think of it, this is the first time I've ever been in Rena's room.  ...I'm kind of nervous!``
``I had some idea, but... what a room.  I can't tell if it's chaos or just an incredibly dense collection of things!``
``...And yet she knows exactly where every single one of her treasures is.``
``Still just as impressive as ever.  How does she even accumulate all this stuff...  Rena must have been a dog in a past life!  I bet she was forever gathering up shoes.``

``I always assumed girls' rooms were tidier, you know — cute and bright, the way you picture them...  Maybe that's just a delusion men have.  I mean, Rena's room is like this, and Mion's room was nothing but manga stacked everywhere.``
changeReason: The original says 'comics laying around everywhere' while the JPN 漫画だらけ means 'full of manga'; additionally, the new translation uses 'manga' instead of 'comics' which is more accurate for the Japanese context, and 'misconception' vs 'delusion' (幻想 means fantasy/delusion).

TextENGNew: ``Ahahaha, well, this old man isn't exactly a little girl~!``
``...Hmm?  I think that was just Sis's manga room, actually?  Kei-chan, have you never been in Sis's real room?  Not the main house — the separate one a bit further away... mmff!!``
changeReason: The original says 'have you ever been in Sis's actual room' while the new says 'have you never been in Sis's real room'; the JPN 入ったことはないんですか uses a negative question form (haven't you been?), and the new translation also changes 'Huh?' to 'Hmm?' and 'the one farther away' to 'the separate one a bit further away,' with 離れ specifically meaning a detached building.

TextENGNew: ``Sh-Shion!!  Shhhh!!  Shhhh!!!``
``Oh — look over here!  Look at all the books on Rena-san's desk!!``
changeReason: The original says 'Look at all the books over here' which omits that the books are specifically on Rena's desk (レナさんの机の上); the new translation adds 'on Rena-san's desk' matching the JPN.

TextENGNew: Satoko pointed at Rena's study desk. Atop it sat a mountain of books, piled up helter-skelter.
changeReason: The original says 'A big pile of books, some open' while the JPN 読み散らして積み上げた means 'read and scattered/piled up haphazardly'; 'some open' is not in the JPN, and 学習机 is specifically a 'study desk' not just 'desk.'

TextENGNew: ``What kind of books are these?!  Hmm~?  Hardcovers?  Did she borrow these from the library?  Let's see... 'Modern Maid Theory'... 'The Way of the Maid is Not Walked in a Day'... 'What is Beautiful Service.'  ...Wait — they're all books about maids?``
...Hmm?
``W-WHAT THE HEEEECK?!``
